rag_rules: Generate assume clauses for input envelope constraints using visible feature names and provided numeric bounds.
Place all assumptions and guarantees inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks of the target component implementation.
generic_rules: Use explicit numeric bounds from requirements to form logical expressions in assume/guarantee statements.
Only reference identifiers explicitly visible in the AADL model; avoid AADL property names as AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'assume "label": expression;' syntax for input constraints and ensure expressions are well-typed.
Declare auxiliary boolean conditions with 'eq name : bool = ...;' when composing range checks.
anti_patterns: Do not convert Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric comparisons unless the AADL model exposes them as scalars.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE-visible variables.
irrelevant_topics: component type vs implementation annex placement decision
